result.stdout:
Synopsis:
        LightIngest.dll [String] [-managedIdentity:String] [-azCli[:true-or-false]] [-ingestWithManagedIdentity:String] [-database:String] -table:String -sourcePath:String [-ConnectToStorageWithUserAuth:String] [-ConnectToStorageLoginUri:String] [-connectToStorageWithManagedIdentity:String] [-pattern:String] [-prefix:String] [-tag:string*] [-creationTimePattern:String] [-format:String] [-zipPattern:String] [-ignoreFirstRow[:true-or-false]] [-ingestionMappingPath:String] [-ingestionMappingRef:String] [-dontWait[:true-or-false]] [-interactive[:true-or-false]] [-compression:Double] [-noSizeLimit[:true-or-false]] [-limit:Int32] [-listOnly[:true-or-false]] [-ingestTimeout:Int32] [-wamless[:true-or-false]] [-forceSync[:true-or-false]] [-dataBatchSize:Int32] [-filesInBatch:Int32] [-parallelRequests:Int32] [-devTracing:String] [-repeatPause:Int32] [-repeatCount:Int32] [-ingestionRateCount:UInt32] [-listingRateCount:UInt32] [@responseFile]

    Arguments:
        [String]
            Connection string pointing at a Kusto service endpoint,
            For example, "https://ingest-contoso.westus.kusto.windows.net;Fed=true"
            To login as a specific user use "https://ingest-<cluster hostname>.kusto.windows.net;User={UserId};Fed=true".
            To login to a specific tenant authority Id add ';AuthorityId=<tenantId>' to the connection string.".
            To use managed identity to authenticate to Kusto service use the 'managedIdentity' arg.
            To use app + key authentication use "https://ingest-<cluster hostname>.kusto.windows.net;AppClientId=<appid>;AppKey=<key>;AuthorityId=<tenantId>;Fed=true".
            To use app + certificate thumbprint authentication use "https://ingest-<cluster hostname>.kusto.windows.net;AppClientId={appId};Application Certificate Thumbprint={thumbprint};AuthorityId={tenantId};Fed=true".
            To use app + certificate subject distinguished name authentication use "https://ingest-<cluster hostname>.kusto.windows.net;AppClientId={appId};Subject={SubjectName};AuthorityId={tenantId};Fed=true".
            For complete Kusto Connection String documentation, see https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/kusto/api/connection-strings/kusto

        [-managedIdentity:String]
        [-mi:...]
            Client Id of the managed identity (user-assigned or system-assigned) to be used for connecting to Kusto. Use "system" for system-assigned identity.
            The managed identity needs at least 'Table Ingestor' permissions on the target Ksuto table.
            The managed identity needs to be configured in the azure service that currently runs this tool.

        [-azCli[:true-or-false] (default is: False)]
            If set to 'true', uses the Azure CLI to authenticate to the Kusto service. The Azure CLI must be installed and logged in.

        [-ingestWithManagedIdentity:String]
        [-ingestmi:...]
            Object id of the managed identity (user-assigned or system-assigned) to be used by Kusto to download the data. Use "system" for system-assigned identity.
            The managed identity needs 'Read' permissions over the blobs and to be configured in the Kusto service.

        [-database:String]
        [-db:...]
            Name of the Kusto database to receive the data. If set, overrides the database specified in the connection string.

        [Mandatory: True]
        -table:String
            Name of the Kusto table to receive the data.

        [Mandatory: True]
        -sourcePath:String
        [-source:...]
        [-For the complete list see: "https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/data-explorer/kusto/api/connection-strings/storage-connection-strings#storage-connection-string-templates":...]
            When the source data to ingest resides on a local disk, this is the path to the directory where the data is located
            When the source data resides on cloud storage, this is the root URI of the container, adls filesystem or s3 bucket, affixed with an account key or SAS
            For example, "https://ACCOUNT_NAME.blob.core.windows.net/CONTAINER_NAME?SAS_TOKEN" or "https://ACCOUNT_NAME.blob.core.windows.net/CONTAINER_NAME;ACCOUNT_KEY"

        [-ConnectToStorageWithUserAuth:String]
        [-storageUserAuth:...]
            Optionally authenticate to the data source storage service with user credentials for listing of the container. Use this if you cannot configure keys on the sourcePath.
            The identity used here needs at least 'Read' and 'List' privileges on the container.
            Options: 'PROMPT', 'DEVICE_CODE', 'AZ_CLI'
            Default: Off

        [-ConnectToStorageLoginUri:String]
        [-storageLoginUri:...]
            If 'ConnectToStorageWithUserAuth' is not default, optionally provide the AAD login Uri.
            Default: Public Azure Cloud login Uri

        [-connectToStorageWithManagedIdentity:String]
        [-storageMi:...]
            Optionally authenticate to the data source storage service with managed identity credentials.
            Provide here the Client id of the managed identity (user-assigned or system-assigned). Use "system" for system-assigned identity.The managed identity needs to be configured in the azure service that currently runs this tool.

        [-pattern:String]
            A wildcard pattern, such as ‘MyData*.csv’, that indicates which source data files or blobs to ingest. This includes the folder path.

        [-prefix:String]
            When the source data resides on blob storage, this is the URL prefix shared by all files or blobs, excluding the container name.
            For example, 'Dir1/Dir2'

        [-tag:string*]
            A name/value pair (or simply a string) that is attached to the ingested data as ‘extent tags’. This switch can be used multiple times.
            See https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/kusto/management/extents-overview#extent-tagging for details

        [-creationTimePattern:String]
            When set, will be used to extract the CreationTime property from the file/blob path.

        [-format:String]
        [-f:...]
            Data format. For the list of supported formats, consult https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/kusto/management/data-ingestion/#supported-data-formats

        [-zipPattern:String]
            String value indicating the regular expression to use when selecting which files in a ZIP archive to ingest. All other files in the archive will be ignored. For example: -zipPattern:"*.csv"

        [-ignoreFirstRow[:true-or-false]]
        [-ignoreFirst:...]
            Ignores the first row in a file. This is useful when ingesting CSV or similar data that has a header row.

        [-ingestionMappingPath:String]
        [-mappingPath:...]
            Path to the ingestion mapping file. See https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/kusto/management/mappings for details.

        [-ingestionMappingRef:String]
        [-mappingRef:...]
            Pre-created ingestion mapping name to use. See https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/kusto/management/mappings for details.

        [-dontWait[:true-or-false] (default is: False)]
            If set to 'true', does not wait for ingestion completion. Useful when ingesting large amounts of files/blobs.

        [-interactive[:true-or-false] (default is: True)]
        [-i:...]
            If set to 'false', does not prompt for arguments confirmation. For unattended flows and non-interactive environments.

        [-compression:Double]
        [-cr:...]
            Compression ratio hint. Useful when ingesting compressed files/blobs to help Kusto assess the raw data size.

        [-noSizeLimit[:true-or-false] (default is: False)]
            Ignore built-in file size limitations. Will attempt to ingest file of any size.

        [-limit:Int32]
        [-l:...]
            Limit to first N files.

        [-listOnly[:true-or-false]]
        [-list:...]
            Only lists the files/blobs that would have been posted for ingestion, without performing the ingestion itself.

        [-ingestTimeout:Int32 (default is: 60)]
            Timeout (minutes) for the ingest operations. Defaults to 60 minutes.

        [-wamless[:true-or-false] (default is: False)]
        [-nowam:...]
            If set, disabled MSAL Web Account Manager (WAM) functionality

        [-forceSync[:true-or-false]]
            Forces synchronous ingestion for direct ingestion mode

        [-dataBatchSize:Int32]
        [-batch:...]
            Batch size of data ingestion chunks in MBs

        [-filesInBatch:Int32]
            Maximal number of blobs in a single direct ingest command

        [-parallelRequests:Int32]
        [-parallel:...]
            Max parallel direct ingestion requests

        [-devTracing:String]
        [-trace:...]
            If set, write trace logs to a local directory (by default, 'RollingLogs' in the current directory, or can be modified by setting the switch value).

        [-repeatPause:Int32]
        [-pause:...]
            The period of time to pause between repetitions

        [-repeatCount:Int32]
        [-repeat:...]
            Number of repetitions of the whole ingest cycle (0: no repetitions, -1: repeat indefinitely)

        [-ingestionRateCount:UInt32 (default is: 500)]
        [-rateCount:...]
            Number of blobs queued in one second.

        [-listingRateCount:UInt32 (default is: 500)]
        [-listRateCount:...]
            Number of blobs listed in one second.

        [@responseFile]
            Response file is a text file that contain command line arguments. Each argument must be on a separate line. Explicit arguments take precedence over response file arguments.
